title: Neolobivia incaica
intro: Neolobivia incaica, also known as the Inca lily, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, characterized by its vibrant yellow and orange blooms. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, suggesting its utility in treating conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections. Neolobivia incaica contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Neolobivia incaica include infusions, decoctions, and creams, which are used to topically treat skin conditions, promote wound healing, and provide relief from pain and inflammation.
taxonomy: Neolobivia incaica is a species of cactus in the genus Neolobivia. It belongs to the family Cactaceae. Within the cactus family, Neolobivia incaica is part of the subfamily Cactoideae. The subfamily Cactoideae is further classified into several tribes, with Neolobivia incaica being a member of the tribe Notocacteae. Its classification is an important aspect of its identification and study in the scientific community.
